kills: L^infty self-check theorem is UNSOUND and its premise is now refuted by explicit counterexample. The claimed corollary ('no plus-framed L^infty gadget can carry stacking-sufficient deficiency at k=5, else its stack would be a 5-chromatic L^infty-UDG, contradiction') is a non sequitur: the D4 stack of a deficient gadget is 5-colorable and forces the dis pattern, but dis needs only 4 colors on the frame, so the stack can be (and in L^infty, by Chilakamarri, must be) 4-colorable. No contradiction arises. I then found two explicit 4-CHROMATIC n=11 abstract gadgets carrying stacking-sufficient stealth deficiency (missing {Oac,s} and {Obd,s}, dis present, verified by exact full enumeration of all proper 5-colorings), proving 4-colorability does not preclude the deficiency. Consequence: 'deficiency is a strictly Euclidean observable' is unproven and likely false; the L^infty detector pass survives only via the lift-failure and raw-material arguments, not via the claimed fullness law.

K4-free regime null result undermines the breeding prediction. UDG gadgets live in the omega<=3 world, but ALL 13 stealth-sufficient abstract hits I found required dense K4-containing graphs; a targeted probe of 5018 qualifying K4-free random gadgets (n=10-14) found ZERO deficient profiles of any kind (vs ~25% deficiency rate unrestricted). The proposal's own feared failure mode (a width-2 shadow of de Grey's omega<=3 conjecture forcing fullness) now has its first supporting data, and 'deficiency > 0 within ~10^4 candidates' is empirically challenged in exactly the regime that matters.

Endgame distance lock and missing lift step. The plus-frame is the UNIQUE two-unit-edge frame with full D4 symmetry (V4/parallel frames cannot kill a single transporter bit, as the proposal itself notes), so the forced pairs are locked to distance sqrt(2)/2. A realizable clamp at distance d yields chi(R^2)>=6 only via: O(2)-transitivity making d a universally forbidden distance for plane 5-colorings, PLUS a finite 6-chromatic {1, sqrt(2)/2} two-distance witness. The cited L24 (lean/HadwigerNelson/L24TripleCoupling.lean) is an abstract bridge-graph chi>=6 characterization, not this lift; abstract contraction of s=t gives a 6-chromatic ABSTRACT graph, not a UDG. 'A hit ... decides chi >= 6 outright' and 'one SAT call from a clamp verdict' overstate; the ratio-sqrt(2) two-distance lift must be pre-verified (or frames redesigned) before this route can claim a bound.

Conjecture 1's 'equivalently' clause is false as stated: a full 7-bit profile is strictly stronger than 'every simultaneous double merge remains 5-SAT'. Double merges test only the transporter bits (e via L1=R1&L2=R2, s via L1=R2&L2=R1); the four overlap bits require merge-plus-edge probes. The 7-probe selector design in the experiment is correct; the conjecture statement is not.

'A strictly weaker demand than any previously identified sufficient object' is overstated: a direct clamp is ALSO a 2-missing-bit profile (the aligned pairs {e,Oac}, {e,Obd}, {s,Oad}, {s,Obc}), and stealth stacking-sufficiency additionally demands dis present plus plus-frame realization. Neither object implies the other. The genuine gains are the graded 0-7 fitness and a 2x widening of the 2-bit target set (8 patterns vs 4), not a strict weakening.

FACT B 'parallel birth' is profile-lattice algebra only: no graph realizing T1={dis,Oac,Obd,s} or T2={dis,Oad,Obc,e} was exhibited, and the proposal's experimental plan never tests abstract realizability of any stealth profile (its decisive run is expected, by its own Conjecture 1, to return all-full on the lineage). I closed part of this hole myself: stealth stacking-sufficient profiles ARE abstractly graph-realizable at n=11, but only, so far, with K4s.

Conjecture 3's 'if' direction conflicts with known Kempe-equivalence failures (Kempe classes can be disconnected below degeneracy/regularity thresholds; cf. the 3-prism phenomenon and the Bonamy et al. line); an s-coloring can sit in a separate Kempe class while every e-coloring percolates. The proposal concedes this; only the cheap necessary-condition lemma is salvageable as a pre-filter.
prior_art_collisions: Polymath16 virtual edges: forced-different pairs at k=4 used as graph edges are exactly this proposal's clamp concept one level down; two-point orbital profiles at k=5 on the lineage are genuinely unmeasured (L57 tested only single merges and single added edges, confirmed in experiments/LEARNINGS.md), so Conjecture 1's observable is new, but the 'virtual K4' idea is a k=5 upgrade of Polymath16 practice, not unprecedented.

de Grey 2018 / Heule's record graphs: building forcing by overlaying rotated and reflected copies through shared vertex sets is the field's standard move; FACT C is a clean formalization (orbital bookkeeping plus the D4 frame stabilizer), not a new mechanism.

Chilakamarri (chi(L^infty)=4) and Woodall (chi(Q^2)=2): used correctly as controls, except the proposal's L^infty fullness 'theorem' which is unsound (see kills); my bipartite-fullness lemma (any bipartite 2-edge-ported gadget has a full 7-bit profile at k=5, proved by explicit recoloring and confirmed 176/176) is the airtight Q^2 pass the proposal needed.

Exoo-Ismailescu two-distance chromatic results: the endgame needs a finite 6-chromatic {1, sqrt(2)/2} (ratio sqrt(2)) two-distance graph; existing two-distance literature covers specific ratios and should be checked for sqrt(2) before committing to the plus-frame; no such witness is cited in the proposal or found in the repo.

Kempe equivalence literature (Las Vergnas-Meyniel degeneracy bounds, Mohar's conjecture, Bonamy-Bousquet-Feghali-Johnson, Cereceda-van den Heuvel-Johnson): directly covers and likely refutes Conjecture 3's 'if' direction; the lemma direction is folklore-adjacent.

In-repo: builds correctly on L42/L45/L51/L55/L56/L57 (all representations verified against experiments/LEARNINGS.md); does not re-propose any logged kill (parallel/theta composition is genuinely outside the L55 series-chain monoid obstruction, and FACT A extends the series confinement honestly).
result.surviving_kernel: The width-2 orbital port calculus and the D4 plus-frame stacking theorem survive intact: I independently re-derived the 7-orbital decomposition, the 50 stealth profiles, the order-8 frame stabilizer with orbits {e,s} / {Oac,Oad,Obc,Obd} / {dis}, and verified FACT C over all 128 profiles (0 violations). The stacking step is moreover fail-safe in R^2: bulk collisions or unintended unit edges only shrink the port relation, so forcing survives, and loss of 5-colorability of the union would itself be a chi>=6 UDG. The reduction 'W3 = realize a 5-colorable K4-free plus-framed UDG gadget with dis present, one overlap bit and one transporter bit missing' is a correct, strictly sharper restatement of the wide-interface route (ii), with a graded 0-7 SAT fitness that no prior sweep could see. Two new facts from my attack strengthen the kernel: (1) the target profile IS abstractly graph-realizable (explicit n=11 witnesses, exact enumeration), so the channel is non-vacuous; (2) deficiency provably cannot exist in the Q^2 control (bipartite fullness lemma). The two-point lineage profile sweep (Conjecture 1's experiment) is well-posed, cheap, runnable on the repo stack, and measures a genuinely new observable regardless of outcome. What does NOT survive: the claim that deficiency is a strictly Euclidean observable, the 'strictly weaker demand' framing, the 10^4-candidate breeding optimism (challenged by the K4-free null), and the unexamined sqrt(2)/2 endgame lift.
result.cheapest_decisive_test: A K4-free abstract existence search, run BEFORE any lineage sweep or UDG breeding: SAT/enumeration hunt for a 5-colorable K4-FREE graph (n up to ~25; seeds: near-critical K4-free 5-chromatic graphs, L51-style splits of Mycielski towers, dense triangle-rich K4-free random graphs) with two marked disjoint edges, all four cross pairs non-adjacent, whose 7-bit profile misses exactly one transporter and one overlap bit with dis present (e.g. missing {s,Obd}). My probes already show: unrestricted graphs yield such gadgets at n=11 within minutes, but 5018 qualifying K4-free samples yield zero deficient bits. If targeted search shows K4-free stealth deficiency is absent (a width-2 shadow of omega<=3), the clamp factory dies one level before geometry and the proposal's breeding phase is pointless; if a K4-free witness exists, it becomes the concrete W3 realizability target and the program proceeds. Hours of compute on the existing python-sat stack; decides the channel's viability.
result.steelman: Strongest defensible version: (1) Keep the verified calculus: at k=5 a two-edge-port relation is a union of the 7 S_5-orbitals; series chains of stealth profiles can never force (FACT A, extending L55), while theta-gluing both ports intersects profiles, and on the plus-frame the full D4 stabilizer stacks any profile containing dis and missing one overlap plus one transporter down to exactly {dis}, a virtual K4 on two unit edges that is fail-safe under bulk collisions (any collision-induced 5-UNSAT is itself a chi>=6 UDG). (2) State the target honestly: the missing object is a 5-colorable, K4-FREE, unit-distance-realizable plus-framed gadget with a 2-bit misaligned deficiency; this is a parallel sibling of the direct clamp (both are 2-bit objects), whose value is a graded SAT fitness invisible to all width-1 sweeps, not a strictly weaker demand. (3) Replace the false L^infty theorem with the correct detector statement: deficiency itself is norm-blind (4-chromatic deficient gadgets exist), and the Euclidean content enters only at W3 realizability and at the O(2)-transitivity lift; in L^infty the lift fails (8 preserved directions) and in Q^2 deficiency is provably impossible (bipartite fullness lemma). (4) Order the experiments by decisiveness: first the K4-free abstract existence search (kills or arms the channel), then the two-point lineage profile sweep (decides Conjecture 1, sharpens the wall to 2-point fullness or hands over a precursor), then deficiency-guided breeding seeded by any K4-free abstract witness. (5) Before locking the geometry, verify the endgame: either locate or construct a finite 6-chromatic {1, sqrt(2)/2} two-distance witness for the forbidden-distance lift, or extend FACT C to single-reflection frames (2-copy stacks needing 3 missing bits) to free the forced-pair distance d and aim it at a ratio where the two-distance lift is already known. Keep Conjecture 3 only as the cheap necessary-condition Kempe pre-filter.
